title: Public data entity + security privilege/duty/role chain over a table
instruction: In the sandbox model, expose ConDemoNoteHeader (NoteId, Subject) for OData/DMF integration and lock it behind a proper security chain. Create: (1) a public data entity DemoNoteHeaderEntity (EntityCategory=Master, mapping NoteId/Subject to the table, primary key on NoteId); (2) a display menu item DemoNoteHeaderDisplay pointing at the DemoNoteHeaderList form; (3) a security privilege DemoNoteHeaderMaintain at maintain access level, granting EntryPoints access to the menu item AND DataEntityPermissions access to the data entity; (4) a security duty DemoNoteHeaderDuty grouping the privilege; (5) a security role DemoNoteHeaderRole grouping the duty. Use get_knowledge(topic="data entities") and get_knowledge(topic="security") to ground the patterns, and security_info to sanity-check the chain. All five objects must build clean, and the chain must actually resolve end to end (a non-empty privilege with real entry points/data-entity grants, a non-empty duty, a non-empty role) -- an empty skeleton that merely compiles is not correct.
